codes.description: WGSL source failed deterministic parse
WGSL semantic analysis failed before IR lowering
Shared shader IR construction failed
Shared shader IR validation failed
Legacy AST-direct WGSL to MSL transformation failed
IR to MSL emission failed
MSL compilation failed before metallib packaging
Metal library packaging failed
IR to SPIR-V emission failed
SPIR-V validation failed
IR to DXIL emission failed
Native DXIL backend is not available on this lane
DXIL validation failed
Manifest hash chain mismatch
Shader stage is not compute; CSL lane only accepts compute entry points. Emitted at runtime/zig/src/doe_wgsl/emit_csl_classify.zig when KernelPattern.classify observes a non-compute entry.
Compute kernel references a WGSL builtin (textures, atomics, derivatives) with no CSL equivalent. Emitted at runtime/zig/src/doe_wgsl/emit_csl_classify.zig when hasUnsupportedBuiltins returns true.

Compute kernel did not match any known CSL lowering pattern (gather, reduce, matmul, softmax, attention, kv-read, fused-ffn). Emitted at runtime/zig/src/doe_wgsl/emit_csl_classify.zig when no specific pattern match succeeds.

IR pattern accepted by the CSL classifier but the lowering pass could not produce HostPlan or CSL artifacts for it. General catch-all for classifier/lowering disagreement; prefer a more specific csl_unsupported_* code when the failure mode is known.

cslc rejected a compile targeting the deprecated CSELFRunner runtime. Detected when cslc stderr matches 'n_channels=0 corresponds to the deprecated runtime CSELFRunner'. Driver fix: pass --channels=N with N>=1 to route through SdkRuntime.

cslc requires explicit --fabric-offsets for memcpy-mode compiles. Detected when cslc stderr matches 'The core must have --fabric-offsets='. Driver fix: derive offsets=(4+width_west_buf,1) from the plan's peGrid.

Emitted CSL declares a `param X: T;` without a default value and the host didn't supply it via --params. Compiler semantic: 'only var and extern const variables may be uninitialized'. Emitter or driver fix depending on the param.
layout.csl @export_name(... , T1, mutable) does not match the pe_program.csl @export_symbol(<name>_ptr) pointer element type. Emitter fix: keep layout export type and pe_program var type in lockstep.

Emitted CSL references an identifier that was not declared. Commonly an @export_symbol(<name>_ptr) where <name>_ptr is missing, or a body reference to a WGSL-only identifier (gid, arrayLength) not translated. Emitter fix.

lld ran out of PE memory for the emitted program. Emitted CSL per-PE footprint exceeds the WSE PE memory budget (~48 KB data + task tables). Kernel/emitter fix: shrink chunk_size, split the workload across more PEs, or stream weights rather than resident.

cslc rejected an expression because the inferred type differed from the expected type (e.g. 'expected type u32, got: i16'). Commonly from mixing param-typed constants with u32 indices without @as(u32, ...). Emitter fix.
cslc rejected a function/builtin call with the wrong number of arguments. Emitter fix.

cslc parser rejected a token — 'Unexpected character at this location'. Commonly caused by unsupported type syntax such as `[N][M]T` array-of-vector declarations; canonical shape is flat `[N*M]scalar`. Emitter fix.

The configured Cerebras SDK cslc may wrap a SIF container. The host needs singularity or apptainer on PATH when that runner mode is used. Detected when cslc stderr matches 'singularity not in $PATH'. Host preflight should verify apptainer/singularity presence.

cslc exited non-zero but the stderr did not match any known SDK failure pattern. The full stderr log path is preserved in driver-result.compile.targets[].stderrPath and is the canonical evidence. Add a new explicit code to _CSLC_FAILURE_PATTERNS when a pattern is confirmed.

Two or more compile targets in the same lane failed with different csl_compile_* classifications. The summary.failureCodes[] field lists the distinct codes; per-target codes live in driver-result.compile.targets[].failureCode. Fix each target individually.

doe_csl.description: Every Doppler/WGSL operator routed toward the CSL lane must terminate in exactly one of three outcomes: (1) supported CSL pattern with emitted HostPlan and CSL artifacts; (2) explicit csl_unsupported_* receipt carrying a code from `unsupportedCodes`; (3) hard compiler or runtime failure whose code matches one of the prefixes in `failureCodePatterns` (populated after SDK diagnostics surface the concrete failure shapes). No fourth path: no Cerebras-only fork, no silent fallback, no directional promotion.
